behavior_summary: Responds to a hit (U8P_CLRWATER::gotHit event) by spawning a WATER behavior/process (WATER::0080) to react to the impact — likely to clear or update water at or near the hit location.
Before spawning it configures spawn context via engine ops 'set info' and 'process exclude' which affect the spawned process's metadata and concurrency/exclusion rules.
Passes several arguments to the spawned WATER::0080 process (including values derived from BP+06h, BP+0Ah, BP+0Ch, pid and a constant 0x020C).

Inspects the spawn return value, applies a logical 'implies' operation (with literal operands 1,1), stores that result into a temp local, then suspends/returns; the implication result likely serves as a boolean/flow indicator but its exact use is not visible in this snippet.
notable_control_flow: Call sequence: set info -> process exclude -> spawn WATER::0080 with prepared arguments.
Immediately after spawn, the returned value is pushed/consumed by an 'implies' operation (operands include the retval and literals 1,1), result stored to a temp local.
Function then suspends/returns; no observed global flags are modified in this fragment.
open_questions: What exact semantics and parameter meanings does WATER::0080 implement (clear water, play splash animation, update water tiles, etc.)?
What are the precise meanings of the pushed arguments (especially BP+06h passed twice and the indirect 2-byte value) in relation to water-clearing behavior?
Which info key/value is being set by 'set info' and how does it influence the spawned WATER process?
How is the boolean-like result of the 'implies' operation used by the larger game system (the temp is stored but not used in this excerpt)?

main_function.name: U8P_CLRWATER::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle a hit event by configuring spawn context and spawning the WATER::0080 process to perform a water-related reaction near the impacted object/location; then interpret the spawn's return value to a boolean-like temp and suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 6 (U8P_CLRWATER::gotHit) — invoked when this object is struck/hit

function_map.name: U8P_CLRWATER::gotHit
function_map.role: Respond to hit events by spawning WATER::0080 with contextual parameters, using set info/process exclude to prepare spawn context, and post-processing the spawn retval.

external_calls.purpose: Configure metadata/state that will apply to or parameterize the subsequent spawn (affects how the spawned process sees the target or its behavior).
Set exclusion rules or concurrency constraints influencing the upcoming spawn or related processes (prevents conflicting processes or marks the target excluded).

Spawn a WATER-handling process to react to the hit — inferred to clear or otherwise update water at/near the impact using passed parameters (0x020C, BP+06h, an indirect 2-byte value, pid, BP+0Ch, BP+0Ah).
Apply a logical implication operation to the spawn return value (with literal operands 1,1) to produce a boolean-like result stored in a temp local for later use or signaling.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Responds to a hit (U8P_CLRWATER::gotHit event) by spawning a WATER behavior/process (WATER::0080) to react to the impact — likely to clear or update water at or near the hit location.
